title: Beef Fajitas
description: Sizzling marinated flank steak with charred peppers and onions, served on warm soft flour tortillas.

ingredients.note: red, yellow, or green, sliced into strips

ingredients.item: sour cream, guacamole, shredded cheese, salsa, lime wedges, fresh cilantro

steps.instruction: Prepare marinade: whisk together 2 tbsp olive oil, lime juice, garlic, and all spices in a shallow dish or zip-lock bag.
Add steak to marinade. Turn to coat. Marinate at room temperature for 30 minutes, or covered in the refrigerator for up to 4 hours.
Heat a cast iron skillet or grill pan over high heat until very hot and just beginning to smoke.
Remove steak from marinade and pat lightly dry. Cook steak 4–5 minutes per side for medium. Do not move the steak while cooking — let it sear undisturbed.
Remove steak from pan. Rest on a cutting board for 5–8 minutes — this is essential for juicy meat.
While steak rests, add remaining 1 tbsp olive oil to the same pan over medium-high heat. Add peppers and onions with a pinch of salt. Sauté 5–7 minutes until soft and lightly charred.
Slice steak across the grain into thin strips. This is critical for tenderness.
Warm tortillas: wrap in a damp paper towel and microwave for 30–45 seconds, or warm directly in a dry skillet for 20 seconds per side.
Serve steak and vegetables on warm tortillas with desired toppings.

notes: Always slice steak against the grain — cutting across the muscle fibers makes the meat significantly more tender.
